threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when a bus driver shouts out street names like it's a game, while the rest of us just nod along.
Last night on the 59, a couple tried to convince the driver they missed their stop just to get a free ride back. Classic Brixton hustle.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One afternoon, I saw a kid in a Spider-Man costume directing traffic near the Bötzowstraße playground. That's when I felt it—the heartbeat of Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bar that serves drinks in old-school VHS tapes. It's weirdly nostalgic and totally Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they dodge the street performers on Valencia, but pull out their cash for the taco truck on 16th like it’s a ritual.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with an entire surfboard on the 49 this morning. He said he was 'taking it to the ocean.' Classic San Francisco moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell but miss the magic in our local squares. The real vibe is in the hidden plazas where we sip vermut and watch life unfold.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: Those wide-eyed stares at the metro map, as if it’s an ancient scroll. They always skip past L3, searching for 'the green line' instead.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You try to fit in at the local cafes but still order a flat white like it’s a normal coffee, while everyone’s downing oat milk lattes.
Caught the 59 last night; two blokes debating the best chicken spots in Brixton like it was the World Cup. Made the ride feel like a local pub chat.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I found the old lady at the corner bakery talking to the pigeons like they’re her best friends. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, you'll find the wildest art in the most unexpected places—like the graffiti-covered U-Bahn tunnels. Each visit feels like a new gallery tour.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they shrug off a BART shutdown, but get mad over a delayed Muni bus for their avocado toast run.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a kid on a skateboard riding next to a Muni bus, both moving at a snail's pace. Only in SF do they seem to race for the same destination.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm hides in the local festivals and spontaneous street concerts that come alive after dark.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still take photos of every street sign and rave about the architecture like it's their first time seeing a building.
